title: Playbook - BitSight Findings Retrieval
applies_to: Retrieve BitSight findings by company entity and risk vector
prerequisites: Entity GUID for the target company or a prior company-discovery step
Selected risk vector or findings class
required_permissions: BitSight token with access to the target entity and findings data
Prefer company API tokens for service workflows
request_sequence: Authenticate with HTTP Basic Auth using the token as username and an empty password
Resolve or supply the target company entity GUID
Call GET /v1/companies/{entity_guid}/findings with a risk_vector filter where appropriate
Normalize findings fields and attach the risk vector as part of your output schema
Handle pagination or follow-up calls according to the specific findings family documentation

confirmed_facts.fact: BitSight uses Basic Auth with token-as-username and empty password.
Findings are exposed for a company entity under paths like GET /v1/companies/{entity_guid}/findings with risk_vector filters.
BitSight uses standard HTTP status codes, including 401 for missing or invalid token and 403 for insufficient permissions.

implementation_notes: Model risk_vector as a first-class filter because BitSight organizes many findings families around it.
Keep company/entity resolution separate from findings retrieval if multiple companies are processed in one run.
Normalize findings classes carefully because different vectors can expose different detail fields.
common_failures: 401 from malformed Basic Auth token usage
403 when the token lacks access to the entity or product area
Schema drift across different risk vectors if all findings are forced into one flat shape without null handling
